regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today ESE 7kt, clear skies, 10% rain probability; light ESE 7kt winds and clear skies support routine embarkation and disembarkation at Nassau while minimizing tendering interruptions for Freeport and Bimini. Over the next 24-48 hours Day 1 calls for ESE 11kt, overcast, 10% rain probability across Nassau, Freeport and the Berry Islands; Day 2 shifts to SE 11kt, rain showers, 16% rain probability for Ocho Rios, Falmouth and Puerto Plata. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 forecasts SSE 11kt, drizzle, 29% rain probability, indicating a trend toward lower cloud and drizzle for Grand Cayman, La Romana, Santo Domingo and Samaná.
regions.western-caribbean: Today elevated heat will affect Cozumel, Costa Maya and Belize City, impacting outdoor shore excursions as Cozumel reports ENE 4kt, partly cloudy, 21% rain probability. Over the next 24-48 hours Cozumel is forecast ENE 8kt with drizzle and 21% rain probability on Day 1 and E 7kt with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability on Day 2, with tendering and embarkation expected to operate under standard heat precautions for Cozumel and Roatan.
regions.los-angeles: Today W 3kt, clear skies and less than 10% rain probability keep embarkation, gangway operations and shore excursions at Los Angeles / San Pedro and Long Beach on schedule. Next 24 to 48 hours, WSW 11kt with partly cloudy and less than 10% rain probability on Day 1 then SW 11kt with overcast and less than 10% rain probability on Day 2 will affect docking maneuvers at Long Beach and San Pedro and prompt bridge deck checks at Marina del Rey. Beyond 48 hours, S 9kt with overcast and less than 10% rain probability on Day 3 indicates lighter southerly winds for Catalina Island, supporting tendering and routine deck operations.
regions.ensenada: Today SSW 2kt and overcast with less than 10% rain probability supports routine embarkation and disembarkation at Ensenada and Punta Banda with gangways stable under light winds. Over the next 24 to 48 hours WSW 8kt and foggy with less than 10% rain probability will affect Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos by reducing visibility and potentially slowing harbor transits and tender operations for shore excursions. Beyond 48 hours a shift to SW 8kt with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability brings a sustained SW wind component that may require adjusted pilotage and berth alignment for Islas Todos Santos and Ensenada.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today SE 7kt winds, partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability support steady gangway operations and permit scheduled shore excursions in Barcelona and Valencia with minimal wind-related tendering concerns. Over the next 24 to 48 hours ESE 7 to 10kt with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability will preserve pool- and deck-based activities and reduce glare for sun-dependent excursions at Palma de Mallorca, Ibiza and Malaga. Beyond 48 hours SSW 10kt with thunderstorms and 12% rain probability could force temporary suspension of open-deck shore excursions and require operational adjustments for Cadiz, Lisbon and Gibraltar as ships plan for localized convective activity.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today SSW 7kt winds, overcast skies and an 18% rain probability support routine embarkation and gangway operations at Piraeus and Athens, though crew should monitor low cloud ceilings for announcements. Next 24–48 hours Day 1 forecasts SSW 9kt with thunderstorms and an 18% rain probability while Day 2 shifts to NNW 9kt, overcast and less than 10% rain probability, with thunderstorms expected around Santorini and Mykonos and overcast holding toward Rhodes and Izmir. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 returns to SSW 6kt, clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, allowing routine tendering and shore excursions in Corfu, Cyprus and Alexandria under light winds.
regions.southeast-alaska: Today S 3kt, drizzle, 66% rain probability is expected to impact port operations and shore excursions in Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway. Next 24–48 hours Day 1 ESE 5kt, rain showers, 66% rain probability is expected to affect embarkation timing in Seattle and dock servicing in Vancouver and Victoria; Day 2 N 5kt, drizzle, less than 10% rain probability applies to Sitka and Haines. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 ESE 7kt, drizzle, 44% rain probability may affect small-boat shore landings at Icy Strait Point and tendering operations in Skagway.
